zotero.abstractNote: We consider the problem of minimizing a finite sum of smooth convex functions over a convex compact set. The Frank-Wolfe (FW) algorithm is a popular choice for this problem as it only requires solving a linear subproblem at each iteration. However, standard FW requires a full gradient evaluation, which is computationally prohibitive in the large-scale setting. We propose a stochastic Frank-Wolfe algorithm that uses variance reduction to achieve a faster convergence rate than existing stochastic FW variants. We provide a convergence analysis for our algorithm and show that it achieves a linear convergence rate for strongly convex functions and a sublinear rate for general convex functions. We also provide empirical evidence on several machine learning problems demonstrating the effectiveness of our approach.

extra.Editors: Hal Daumé III and Aarti Singh

zotero.proceedingsTitle: Proceedings of the 37th International Conference on Machine Learning
